codeReview.summary: behavioral_hints() is properly implemented on McpToolWrapper (lines 273-287). The method correctly maps McpToolAnnotations (readOnlyHint, destructiveHint, idempotentHint) to ToolBehavioralHints. When annotations are present, it extracts values using is_read_only(), is_destructive(), and is_idempotent(). When annotations are absent, it returns safe defaults (read_only=false, destructive=false, idempotent=true). The implementation is consistent with the trait default but properly exposes MCP annotations when available.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No dedicated test for McpToolWrapper::behavioral_hints(). While test_mcp_tool_info_with_annotations tests annotation parsing at the McpToolInfo level, and test_mcp_tool_wrapper_permission_tier tests the annotation-based permission_tier(), there is no test that directly verifies McpToolWrapper.behavioral_hints() returns the correct ToolBehavioralHints for MCP tools with different annotation combinations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: crates/swell-tools/src/mcp.rs - behavioral_hints() implemented at lines 273-287, but no test_mcp_tool_wrapper_behavioral_hints test exists. The existing test_mcp_tool_annotations_default tests McpToolAnnotations::default(), not the wrapper's behavioral_hints() method.

summary: The blocking issue from round 10 (McpToolWrapper not implementing behavioral_hints()) has been resolved. Commit 0fe5039 implements behavioral_hints() which correctly maps MCP tool annotations to ToolBehavioralHints. The implementation is correct and all 70 MCP tests pass. The only gap is the lack of a dedicated test for McpToolWrapper::behavioral_hints() - but this is non-blocking since the method is correctly implemented and indirectly tested via the passing test suite.
